label: vulnerable

source.description: User-controlled string bound from the HTTP query parameter via @RequestParam. No character filter or length limit is applied.
source.code_snippet: public String greet(@RequestParam(defaultValue = "world") String name) throws Exception {

propagation.description: The name value is concatenated with the '+' operator directly into the template source string. User-supplied FreeMarker syntax (e.g. ${7*7}, <#assign>) becomes part of the template to be parsed.

sink.description: new Template(..., new StringReader(templateStr), cfg) parses the attacker-controlled string as a Freemarker template. Any FreeMarker expressions embedded in name are compiled and subsequently evaluated by template.process().
sink.code_snippet: Template template = new Template("greeting", new StringReader(templateStr), cfg);

missing_controls: use a static template constant with ${name} placeholder
pass name as a model variable via template.process(model, out), never concatenate into source
allowlist / regex filter on name if concatenation is unavoidable
expected_findings.risk_reasoning: User input is embedded into the Freemarker template source string before parsing. An attacker supplying '${7*7}' or FreeMarker directives can evaluate arbitrary expressions and potentially achieve RCE via freemarker.template.utility.Execute.
expected_findings.remediation_hint: Define a static template constant 'Hello, ${name}!' and pass name as a model variable: template.process(Map.of("name", name), out). Never concatenate user input into the template source string.

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the propagation point where user input is concatenated into the template source, and the sink where it enters the Freemarker parser.

notes: The Template constructor parses the template source; template.process() evaluates it. The root vulnerability is at the constructor (line 22). Both lines are within the recall_anchor range.
